sections.paragraph:
and

שלה

i.q. שָׁלַל, נָשַׁל to draw out. Hence many derive fut. apoc. יֵשֶׁל (for יִשְׁלֶה), Job 27:8, כִּי יֵשֶׁל אֱלֹוהַּ נַפְשֹׁו “when God draws out his soul,” sc. from his body, as if from the sheath of his soul (comp. נִדְנֶה). So Chald. and Syr.; and this opinion may be acceded to. We should not, however, reject Schnurrer’s conjecture, who supposes ישׁל to be contracted for יִשְׁאַל he asks, compare Arab. يَسءَلُ for يَسْأَلُ, although, if this were adopted, we should apparently read יֵשַׁל.

Derivative, שִׁלְיָה.
